assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 30-33: 'Package: openssl-libs — this is a system-level RPM package, not a source dependency. Ecosystem: RPM. Lock file: rpms.lock.yaml.' The ecosystem is correctly identified as RPM based on the package name openssl-libs, not Cargo.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 11-17: The version impact table includes all five versions — 2.2.0 (v0.4.5, 3.0.7-25.el9_3), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8, 3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9, 3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11, 3.0.7-28.el9_4), and 2.2.4 (v0.4.12, 3.0.7-28.el9_4). Line 6 states: 'All versions in this stream are analyzed using rpms.lock.yaml data at pinned commits.'

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context classifies the package origin as explicit install because openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml, not inherited from the base image (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 29-35: 'openssl-libs is a system-level RPM package present in rpms.lock.yaml. Since it appears in the lock file, this is an explicit install (or explicit lock pin), not inherited solely from the base image. Origin: rpms.lock.yaml (explicit package pin).'

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 11-13: 'Ecosystem: RPM (system package) — only one remediation task is needed. No upstream backport task required since the fix is an RPM update in the Konflux release repo.' Only one jira.create_issue call is present (lines 83-89), targeting the Konflux release repo rhtpa-release.0.4.z.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 29-68: Task description contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 29), '## Target Branch' (line 33), '## Description' (line 37), '## Implementation Notes' (line 57), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 65). No 'Files to Modify' section is present anywhere in the task description, consistent with the remediation-templates.md guidance that this section is intentionally omitted.
